element_introduction_order.element: Vehicle selection (Porsche 959, Ferrari F40, Lamborghini Diablo)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player chooses car and difficulty before any driving begins

element_introduction_order.element: Difficulty setting (determines automatic vs manual transmission)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: affects transmission mechanics and handling throughout course
player learns throttle control, steering, avoiding other cars
player learns to anticipate and avoid police pursuits via speed management

element_introduction_order.first_appears: first encounter when exceeding speed limit
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: crashing into police car ends game immediately; must maintain speed while avoiding collision
staying off-road too long causes damage; crashing adds 30 seconds to time
falling off cliffs results in crash; narrow passages require precision
overrevving engine causes catastrophic failure and loss of life

element_introduction_order.element: Gas station checkpoint (mandatory to avoid fuel loss)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: must reach station or run out of fuel; penalty for missing it is loss of car

typical_relief_triggers: slowing down before police radar detection
finding open road with minimal traffic
typical_tension_triggers: police car appearing and matching player speed
entering mountain tunnel sections with narrow lanes
encountering oncoming traffic at high speeds
approaching cliff edges on mountain levels
fuel gauge running low near end of course
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: escalating pressure: player must maintain high speed to beat time and evade police, but speed increases collision risk and police detection likelihood; relief comes only at course completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Desert Blast (Easy) - flat terrain, cacti obstacles, wide highways allow player to learn basic controls and police evasion without severe elevation hazards
difficulty_curve.mid_game: City Bound (Medium) - varied terrain with multiple car types and moderate turns; introduces sustained navigation complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: East Coast (Hard) and West Coast (Hardest) - extended mountain sections with cliffs, tunnels, narrow passages; mandatory high speeds create unavoidable police confrontation at end
notable_spikes: First police pursuit encounter (immediate game-over threat if player crashes into patrol car)
Transition to mountain courses (cliff obstacles permanently alter failure condition from time penalty to instant crash)
West Coast course (longest course with most complex terrain requiring sustained precision at high speeds)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of four console courses represents a different US region with distinct environmental hazards: desert (simple obstacles), midwest (moderate turns), east coast (elevation), west coast (extreme complexity)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Desert Blast to West Coast progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Game design makes collision-free police evasion nearly impossible at course end due to required speed maintenance; forces player to accept apprehension or risk crash
Early courses feature traffic and trees; later courses add cliffs, tunnels, and narrow mountain passages that create permanent failure states
